items.title: Maruti Suzuki India starts FY27 with increase in market share to 42%
items.summary: The company achieved record domestic sales of 1,91,122 units in April, driven by strong performance in both passenger cars and SUVs. Passenger car sales saw a substantial rise, while SUV sales surged by 141.6%.

items.title: Honda Cars Discounts May 2026 Up To Rs 2.12 Lakh – City, Amaze, Elevate
items.summary: Honda Cars discounts for the month of May 2026 can go till Rs 2.12 lakh and City sedan gets highest cash benefits ahead of facelift launch The post Honda Cars Discounts May 2026 Up To Rs 2.12 Lakh – City, Amaze, Elevate appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: EVs moving at faster speed in premium lane
items.summary: The share of cars below ₹10 lakh has decreased. Luxury EV demand is also strong. This trend suggests the market is expanding upwards, potentially limiting mass adoption.

items.title: Hyundai slips as Tata Motors, Mahindra race ahead in calendar 2026
items.summary: The homegrown carmakers are firing on all cylinders and clearly determined to maintain a healthy lead with expanded portfolios even as the South Korean auto major faces pressure to regain momentum.

items.title: BYD's sales downturn extends to eighth straight month
items.summary: Overseas sales of passenger vehicles and pickup ​trucks, increasingly a source of growth for BYD, jumped 35 per cent to ​130,000 vehicles last month, according to Reuters calculations based on a Weibo post by BYD executive Li Yunfei.

items.title: Steelbird Launches SB-120 Helmet Starting at Rs 3,999
items.summary: Steelbird Helmets has introduced the new SB-120 motorcycle helmet in the Indian market. The new model is priced at Rs 3,999 for the mono color variants and Rs 4,499 for models featuring decals.

items.title: Bajaj Sales April 2026 – Domestic 2.1 Lakh, Exports 2.3 Lakh
items.summary: Bajaj Auto sales in April 2026 were driven by healthy growth across both two wheeler and commercial vehicles segments The post Bajaj Sales April 2026 – Domestic 2.1 Lakh, Exports 2.3 Lakh appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: EVs ring in new fiscal on strong note
items.summary: Electric cars and two-wheelers saw significant year-on-year growth. This surge was fueled by new model introductions, increased consumer interest, and worries about fuel prices. While sales dipped from March's peak, the overall trend indicates strong momentum for electric mobility in the country.

items.title: Dhoot Transmission acquires two- and three-wheeler electronics supplier Multilink
items.summary: The deal strengthens Dhoot’s electronics portfolio amid rising vehicle electrification and EV adoption.

items.title: Atul Auto, Exponent Energy partner to roll out 15-minute charging electric three-wheelers
items.summary: The tie-up will integrate 15-minute rapid charging technology into factory-built vehicles, aiming to accelerate EV adoption in the commercial segment.

items.title: Mahindra dethrones Tata Motors to become top EV revenue player in FY26
items.summary: Tata Motors still leads in the number of electric cars sold. Overall electric car registrations saw a significant jump. New EV models are expected to drive further growth.

items.title: Chinese EV battery maker CATL launches $5 billion Hong Kong share sale
items.summary: CATL has launched a roughly $5 billion share sale in Hong Kong, aiming to capitalize on its strong stock performance and secure funding for global expansion.

items.title: Tata Curvv EV SeriesX Launch Price Starts Rs 16.99 Lakh – 55 kWh, 502 km Range
items.summary: Tata Curvv EV SeriesX launch price is Rs 16.99 lakh (Ex-sh), consisting of Accomplished X and Empowered X trims with 55 kWh battery pack The post Tata Curvv EV SeriesX Launch Price Starts Rs 16.99 Lakh – 55 kWh, 502 km Range appeared first on RushLane .

items.summary: Expected to be imported as CKD units, the iCar V23 will have largely the same profile as the model sold overseas The post Upcoming JSW Chery Electric SUV Spied – 4.2m Long, 69.8 kWh Battery appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: Exclusive: Rocklink Sees ₹300 Crore India Recycling Revenue Opportunity in 5 Years
items.summary: German recycling firm Rocklink is targeting ₹250 crore to ₹300 crore in revenue from its India operations over the next five years, following the commissioning of its first integrated recycling facility in the country. The company has invested around ₹30 crore in the Sikandrabad, Uttar Pradesh-based plant, which will process lithium-ion batteries, rare earth magnets and metal-bearing industrial waste as part of it...

items.title: AMNS India Commissions Automotive Steel Production Line at Hazira Plant
items.summary: ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel India on April 30 inaugurated a new Pickling Line and Tandem Cold Mill (PLTCM) at its Hazira facility in Gujarat, adding 2 million tonnes per annum of capacity to its automotive steel operations. The unit was inaugurated by Keiichi Ono and will produce cold-rolled base steel for automotive applications, including Advanced High Strength Steel (AHSS), galvanised (GI), galvannealed (GA), an...

items.title: Tsuyo Manufacturing secures two EV motor patents; strengthens India’s powertrain R&D capabilities
items.summary: The new patents cover a non-magnetic bridge synchronous reluctance motor and a fault-tolerant five-phase permanent magnet motor with dual inverter control.

items.title: Epsilon Launches Gen III LFP Cathode Material, Targeting US Battery Supply Chains
items.summary: Epsilon Cathode Active Materials (ECAM) announced on April 27 the commercial development of its third-generation lithium iron phosphate (LFP) cathode material, claiming performance metrics that place it alongside Chinese-produced alternatives that have long held a near-monopoly in the global LFP supply chain. The announcement marks one of the few instances of a non-Chinese company bringing high-energy-density LFP...

items.title: Epsilon CAM unveils Gen III LFP battery material, targets global EV supply chain shift
items.summary: Epsilon CAM’s new Gen III LFP cathode material could strengthen alternative battery supply chains as global automakers seek non-Chinese sources for EV components.

items.title: Horse Powertrain plans India entry, explores local manufacturing as small-car engine demand rises
items.summary: The move comes after India’s GST cut on small cars boosted demand for efficient 1.2-litre engines, creating fresh opportunities for global powertrain suppliers.
